title: Follower table stuffing: unsigned Follow inflates delivery list
description:
server.rs:594-608: A Follow activity with no Signature header (see broadside-0e74.1) causes a DB insert into the followers table. The actor_uri and inbox_uri are taken directly from the Follow body and the fetched actor document.

Attack:
- POST /inbox with Follow activities claiming arbitrary actor URIs
- Broadside inserts up to one row per unique (persona_id, actor_uri) pair (INSERT OR IGNORE avoids exact duplicates)
- But actor_uri is attacker-controlled: rotate the actor_uri path slightly each request and insert unlimited rows
- Broadside's delivery worker will attempt to POST to every inbox_uri in the followers table for every published post

Effect:
1. Storage exhaustion: O(requests) rows inserted into followers
2. Outbound amplification: every future post triggers delivery attempts to attacker-controlled inboxes (open relay)
3. DDoS proxy: broadside fans out its signed requests to victim servers listed as inbox_uri

Rate limiter is bypassable (broadside-0e74.7) so volume is not constrained.

Fix: require valid signature before inserting any follower.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
